state.input: Suggest me places and hotels to visit during my time in new york for 3 days under 1000 usd
itinerary: Morning sightseeing at free landmarks.
Afternoon stroll in public parks.
Evening local market time and street food.
Night rest in budget accommodation.

places.name: Statue of Liberty
places.snippet: Iconic symbol of freedom and democracy, offering guided tours and panoramic views from Liberty Island.

places.name: Central Park
places.snippet: Expansive urban park featuring walking paths, lakes, gardens, and recreational facilities in the heart of Manhattan.

places.name: Times Square
places.snippet: Vibrant commercial and entertainment hub known for its bright lights, Broadway theaters, and bustling atmosphere.

places.name: Empire State Building
places.snippet: Historic skyscraper with observation decks providing stunning views of New York City and beyond.

places.name: Metropolitan Museum of Art
places.snippet: World-renowned art museum housing an extensive collection spanning over 5,000 years of culture and history.

places.name: Broadway Theatre District
places.snippet: Famous for premiering top theatrical performances and musicals, offering a quintessential New York experience.

places.name: 9/11 Memorial & Museum
places.snippet: Moving tribute to the victims of September 11, 2001, with exhibits detailing the events and aftermath.

places.name: Brooklyn Bridge
places.snippet: Iconic suspension bridge connecting Manhattan and Brooklyn, popular for walking and cycling with scenic views.

places.name: Fifth Avenue
places.snippet: Famous shopping street featuring luxury boutiques, flagship stores, and landmarks like St. Patrick's Cathedral.

places.name: Museum of Modern Art (MoMA)
places.snippet: Leading museum showcasing an impressive collection of modern and contemporary artworks by renowned artists.

state.input: Suggest me places and hotels to visit during my time in new york for 3 days under 1000 usd
itinerary: Morning sightseeing at free landmarks.
Afternoon stroll in public parks.
Evening local market time and street food.
Night rest in budget accommodation.

restaurants.name: Katz's Delicatessen
restaurants.snippet: $ — Famous for its classic pastrami sandwiches and traditional New York Jewish deli experience.

restaurants.name: Di Fara Pizza
restaurants.snippet: $$ — Legendary Brooklyn pizzeria known for authentic, handcrafted thin-crust pizzas.

restaurants.name: Joe's Shanghai
restaurants.snippet: $$ — Known for their soup dumplings and authentic Shanghai-style Chinese cuisine.

restaurants.name: Levain Bakery
restaurants.snippet: $ — Famous for some of the best thick and gooey chocolate chip cookies in NYC.

restaurants.name: The Halal Guys
restaurants.snippet: $ — Iconic street cart offering delicious halal platters with chicken, gyro, and rice.

restaurants.name: Momofuku Noodle Bar
restaurants.snippet: $$$ — David Chang's renowned spot for innovative ramen and pork buns.

restaurants.name: Ess-a-Bagel
restaurants.snippet: $ — Classic New York bagel shop known for large, fluffy bagels and cream cheese spreads.

restaurants.name: Lucali
restaurants.snippet: $$$ — Cozy Brooklyn pizza place famous for thin-crust, wood-fired pies.

restaurants.name: Smorgasburg
restaurants.snippet: $ — Open-air food market featuring diverse, affordable food vendors and local eats.

restaurants.name: Shake Shack
restaurants.snippet: $ — Popular fast-casual spot known for juicy burgers, crinkle-cut fries, and shakes.

state.input: Suggest me places and hotels to visit during my time in new york for 3 days under 1000 usd
itinerary: Morning sightseeing at free landmarks.
Afternoon stroll in public parks.
Evening local market time and street food.
Night rest in budget accommodation.

hotels.name: HI New York City Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $30-$60 per night — A popular hostel in Manhattan offering dorm-style accommodations with clean facilities and a social atmosphere.

hotels.name: The Jane Hotel
hotels.snippet: hotel · $70-$100 per night — A historic hotel with compact, ship cabin-inspired rooms in the West Village, offering affordability and charm.

hotels.name: Pod 51 Hotel
hotels.snippet: hotel · $80-$120 per night — Modern, compact rooms in Midtown East with a social rooftop and budget-friendly prices.

hotels.name: Chelsea International Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $40-$80 per night — Affordable dorm rooms and private rooms located near Chelsea Market and the High Line.

hotels.name: The Bowery House
hotels.snippet: hostel · $70-$110 per night — A trendy hostel featuring capsule-style rooms in the Nolita neighborhood, perfect for budget travelers.

hotels.name: LIC Hotel
hotels.snippet: hotel · $70-$100 per night — Budget hotel in Long Island City with easy subway access to Manhattan.

hotels.name: NY Moore Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $50-$90 per night — A boutique hostel in Brooklyn offering dorm and private rooms with a friendly vibe.

hotels.name: Broadway Hotel and Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $60-$90 per night — Affordable accommodations in Upper West Side featuring both dormitory beds and private rooms.

state.input: Suggest me places and hotels to visit during my time in new york for 3 days under 1000 usd
itinerary: Day 1: See Statue of Liberty, Central Park — Eat at Shake Shack, Ess-a-Bagel — Stay: HI New York City Hostel
Day 2: See Times Square, Empire State Building — Eat at Katz's Delicatessen, Joe's Shanghai — Stay: The Jane Hotel
Day 3: See Metropolitan Museum of Art, Broadway Theatre District — Eat at Momofuku Noodle Bar, Levain Bakery — Stay: Pod 51 Hotel
Day 4: See 9/11 Memorial & Museum, Brooklyn Bridge — Eat at Lucali, Di Fara Pizza — Stay: Chelsea International Hostel
Day 5: See Fifth Avenue, Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) — Eat at The Halal Guys, Smorgasburg — Stay: The Bowery House

state.finalResponse:
# New York Travel Plan

**Budget:** $1000
> **Current weather:** 18°C · wind 20.1 km/h

## Day-by-Day Itinerary

### Day 1
**See:**
- [Statue of Liberty](https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Statue%20of%20Liberty%20New%20York)

state.input: Suggest me places and hotels to visit during my time in new york for 3 days under 1000 usd
itinerary: Day 1: See Statue of Liberty, Central Park — Eat at Shake Shack, Ess-a-Bagel — Stay: HI New York City Hostel
Day 2: See Times Square, Empire State Building — Eat at Katz's Delicatessen, Joe's Shanghai — Stay: The Jane Hotel
Day 3: See Metropolitan Museum of Art, Broadway Theatre District — Eat at Momofuku Noodle Bar, Levain Bakery — Stay: Pod 51 Hotel
Day 4: See 9/11 Memorial & Museum, Brooklyn Bridge — Eat at Lucali, Di Fara Pizza — Stay: Chelsea International Hostel
Day 5: See Fifth Avenue, Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) — Eat at The Halal Guys, Smorgasburg — Stay: The Bowery House

places.name: Statue of Liberty
places.snippet: Iconic symbol of freedom and democracy, offering guided tours and panoramic views from Liberty Island.

places.name: Central Park
places.snippet: Expansive urban park featuring walking paths, lakes, gardens, and recreational facilities in the heart of Manhattan.

places.name: Times Square
places.snippet: Vibrant commercial and entertainment hub known for its bright lights, Broadway theaters, and bustling atmosphere.

places.name: Empire State Building
places.snippet: Historic skyscraper with observation decks providing stunning views of New York City and beyond.

places.name: Metropolitan Museum of Art
places.snippet: World-renowned art museum housing an extensive collection spanning over 5,000 years of culture and history.

places.name: Broadway Theatre District
places.snippet: Famous for premiering top theatrical performances and musicals, offering a quintessential New York experience.

places.name: 9/11 Memorial & Museum
places.snippet: Moving tribute to the victims of September 11, 2001, with exhibits detailing the events and aftermath.

places.name: Brooklyn Bridge
places.snippet: Iconic suspension bridge connecting Manhattan and Brooklyn, popular for walking and cycling with scenic views.

places.name: Fifth Avenue
places.snippet: Famous shopping street featuring luxury boutiques, flagship stores, and landmarks like St. Patrick's Cathedral.

places.name: Museum of Modern Art (MoMA)
places.snippet: Leading museum showcasing an impressive collection of modern and contemporary artworks by renowned artists.

hotels.name: HI New York City Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $30-$60 per night — A popular hostel in Manhattan offering dorm-style accommodations with clean facilities and a social atmosphere.

hotels.name: The Jane Hotel
hotels.snippet: hotel · $70-$100 per night — A historic hotel with compact, ship cabin-inspired rooms in the West Village, offering affordability and charm.

hotels.name: Pod 51 Hotel
hotels.snippet: hotel · $80-$120 per night — Modern, compact rooms in Midtown East with a social rooftop and budget-friendly prices.

hotels.name: Chelsea International Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $40-$80 per night — Affordable dorm rooms and private rooms located near Chelsea Market and the High Line.

hotels.name: The Bowery House
hotels.snippet: hostel · $70-$110 per night — A trendy hostel featuring capsule-style rooms in the Nolita neighborhood, perfect for budget travelers.

hotels.name: LIC Hotel
hotels.snippet: hotel · $70-$100 per night — Budget hotel in Long Island City with easy subway access to Manhattan.

hotels.name: NY Moore Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $50-$90 per night — A boutique hostel in Brooklyn offering dorm and private rooms with a friendly vibe.

hotels.name: Broadway Hotel and Hostel
hotels.snippet: hostel · $60-$90 per night — Affordable accommodations in Upper West Side featuring both dormitory beds and private rooms.

restaurants.name: Katz's Delicatessen
restaurants.snippet: $ — Famous for its classic pastrami sandwiches and traditional New York Jewish deli experience.

restaurants.name: Di Fara Pizza
restaurants.snippet: $$ — Legendary Brooklyn pizzeria known for authentic, handcrafted thin-crust pizzas.

restaurants.name: Joe's Shanghai
restaurants.snippet: $$ — Known for their soup dumplings and authentic Shanghai-style Chinese cuisine.

restaurants.name: Levain Bakery
restaurants.snippet: $ — Famous for some of the best thick and gooey chocolate chip cookies in NYC.

restaurants.name: The Halal Guys
restaurants.snippet: $ — Iconic street cart offering delicious halal platters with chicken, gyro, and rice.

restaurants.name: Momofuku Noodle Bar
restaurants.snippet: $$$ — David Chang's renowned spot for innovative ramen and pork buns.

restaurants.name: Ess-a-Bagel
restaurants.snippet: $ — Classic New York bagel shop known for large, fluffy bagels and cream cheese spreads.

restaurants.name: Lucali
restaurants.snippet: $$$ — Cozy Brooklyn pizza place famous for thin-crust, wood-fired pies.

restaurants.name: Smorgasburg
restaurants.snippet: $ — Open-air food market featuring diverse, affordable food vendors and local eats.

restaurants.name: Shake Shack
restaurants.snippet: $ — Popular fast-casual spot known for juicy burgers, crinkle-cut fries, and shakes.

costBreakdown.notes: Prices based on budget hostels or guesthouses, meals at local diners or fast-casual spots, unlimited metro/bus day passes, and entry to popular attractions like museums with discount or city passes.
